title: Appendix Ω: Critical Mass of the Local Big Bang as a Coordination Phase Transition, Global Rotation of the Universe, and Its New Minimum Age from the Dipole Turning Radius
description: The Yakushev Unified Coordination Theory (YUCT) posits that our observed Universe originated as a local phase transition—a “Big Bang”—within an eternally rotating, larger cosmos. This Omega document unifies three foundational pillars of YUCT: (1) the derivation of the critical mass M_crit that triggers the local Big Bang from the core postulates; (2) the inflationary phase as a coordination phase transition, including unique observational signatures; and (3) the global rotation of the Universe as the origin of the CMB dipole and a new cosmic age bound. From the YPSDC definition of coordination efficiency and the fractal nature of the coordination network, we prove the scaling law K_eff ∝ R^{β d_f/2} with β = 2/3 and fractal dimension d_f = 2.2. Applying this to a gravitationally bound pre‑collapse region yields M_crit = 3 M_Pl ≈ 6.5×10^{-8} kg. The same postulates govern the inflationary dynamics, predicting n_s ≈ 0.965, r ≈ 0.07, and a distinctive non‑Gaussianity f_NL^local ≈ 1.12. The global rotation of the Universe, inferred from the growth of the CMB dipole with redshift, leads to a rotational period T_rot ≈ 2.3×10^{14} yr, vastly exceeding the standard cosmic age. The consistency of these three independent lines of evidence constitutes a proof of consilience for YUCT. The document presents detailed derivations, falsifiable predictions, and explicit refutation criteria for each component.

structural_topology.description: This directory contains the multilingual versions (EN, RU, DE, ZH, AR, JA, KO) of the paper 'Critical Mass of the Local Big Bang as a Coordination Phase Transition, Global Rotation of the Universe, and Its New Minimum Age from the Dipole Turning Radius'. The root main file is the English version, while the other languages are full translations of the same content.

empirical_law_universality.beta_evidence: Over 12 independent systems (atomic, biological, geophysical, cosmological) confirm β = 2/3 with uncertainties < 0.05
empirical_law_universality.domains_covered: HF–HI bond energies, vertebrate mutation rates, plant root productivity, mammalian basal metabolic rate, gene expression, earthquake b-value, impact crater size distribution, anomalous diffusion, fragmentation, glass relaxation, CMB scalar spectral index

custom.notes: This is the complete multilingual documentation of Appendix Ω, version 2.1. Each language file (EN, RU, DE, ZH, AR, JA, KO) is a full translation of the entire text, including all mathematical derivations, tables, figures, and references. The English version is the original reference. The Zenodo community for YUCT is 'yuct'. The keywords explicitly include '0.67' and 'β = 2/3' for search optimization.
